summary: Multi Monitor Setup on ArchLinux (Wayland, KDE Plasma) can cause popup windows to position incorrectly.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4 and up

### System information

Garuda Linux, Godot 4.4+ 

### Issue description

The application drop down menu's position incorrectly on my center monitor when the side monitors are smaller and aligned via the bottom instead of the top.

They will instead position to match the top of the adjacent monitor instead of the active one. 

This is a relatively minor issue but I can't really re-configure my displays without causing other issues and 4.3 and earlier don't do this. Accessing menu's so far from where they should be is awkward and causes a lot of menu auto-collapsing when I leave the menu's bounds because it's so out of position.


### Steps to reproduce

Default arrangement shown here.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a2618c60-4cec-45fd-a262-48ba3627a47c)


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d11578d-1465-4f35-b0e0-79110fa59feb)
- Here you can see the drop down aligning with the taskbar on the left monitor instead of the active monitor.

When I reconfigure my displays like so, the menus spawn in the correct position.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/043c6a09-ea34-44ca-bfcc-81129a0a8924)

Both monitors must be at the top line, the menu will default to the height of the lowest top line across all monitors.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Open any Godot 4.4+ version, full screen it on a monitor larger than the adjacent monitors and then access a drop down menu.

### Update
This issue applies to the bottom line of the screen as well. When I have my monitors adjusted so the Scene/Project/Debug menu's are working, drop downs in the Output/Debugger/Audio/Animation area are shifted upwards to match the bottom line. I imagine the issue is the same but, for the sake of completeness.

summary: FileAccess Error when opening document file on Android. Only works using MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
- Not reproducible in any earlier version because the Native FileDialog for Android was introduced in 4.4
- Happens on both Gradle and non-Gradle builds

### System information

v4.4.1.stable - Samsung Galaxy S23 (SM-S9110) - Android 14

### Issue description

On Android, I want to select a .txt file from the phone storage, using the **native** FileDialog. 
This works fine and I'm able to get the file path correctly (although with a caveat. More info at the end).

> In my test case, the example text file is located in: /storage/emulated/0/Documents/test.txt

Then, I want to load that file using: `FileAccess.open(file_path, FileAccess.READ)`
This is where I get **ERROR 7: ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND** 

This happens when exporting with no permissions. Because [according to android documentation...](https://developer.android.com/training/data-storage/shared/documents-files) my app shouldn't need any file access permission to access that file.

From that page:
> Because the user is involved in selecting the files or directories that your app can access, this mechanism doesn't require any system permissions, and user control and privacy is enhanced.

Instead, when I enable MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission, the file is loaded correctly.
But I definitely don't want to use this permission, because any user installing my app could be questioning why on earth it's asking for full file access 😅 

I would expect the file to load without setting any special permission.
Unless I'm missing something?

---
Something else I don't understand is that, using Native FileDialog, seems like I'm restricted to selecting files only in certain folders, like /Documents or /Download.
If I try to load any file from other folders, it simply doesn't trigger the FileDialog `file_selected` signal (but it still does with MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission enabled) 🤔


### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a hidden FileDialog to your scene, and a button to show it.
2. On the FileDialog, make sure to enable _use_native_dialog_ and set _Access = FileSystem_
3. Bind the FileDialog __on_file_dialog_file_selected_ signal
4. Bind the button __on_button_pressed_ signal
5. Use this code to open the Dialog and Open the file
6. Deploy on Android device and observe

---
This is my code:

```
extends Control

func _ready() -> void:
	OS.request_permissions()


func _on_file_dialog_file_selected(path: String) -> void:
	print("Selected file: ", path)
	print("File exists? ", FileAccess.file_exists(path))
	
	var file = FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.READ)
	if file:
		print(file.get_as_text())
	else:
		print("ERROR opening file: ")
		print(FileAccess.get_open_error())


func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	$FileDialog.popup()

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[FileDialogTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19721975/FileDialogTest.zip)

summary: The dropdown nesting in the editor inspector works incorrectly when groups (and also subgroups by extension) are in a nested property path.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in v4.4.1.

I haven't seen any changes to how the property list is generally displayed mentioned in the changelogs during the transition to v4.3, v4.4, or v4.4.1, so I highly doubt that this is a regression, or at least a relatively recent one.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12019.1028) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

I found that if a property list's paths are structured like this:
```
global_shader_params/Emissive <- Group/Subgroup
global_shader_params/emissive
global_shader_params/emissive_texture
global_shader_params/emissive_color
```

I get really weird nesting dropdowns in the editor, shown here:
![Incorrect nesting in my project. Notice how it groups the properties inside "Global Shader Params" twice.](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4eefe48a-65cb-46ab-8915-e03dae6ec233)

The problem, is that it looks like this:
```
Global Shader Params
  Emissive
    Global Shader Params
      Emissive
      Emissive Texture
      Emissive Color
```

When it should logically be more like this:
```
Global Shader Params
  Emissive
    Emissive
    Emissive Texture
    Emissive Color
```

### Steps to reproduce

Pretty easy to recreate this bug. Just put a property with the `PROPERTY_USAGE_GROUP` or `PROPERTY_USAGE_SUBGROUP` into a nested path, and it should output a similar result. Here's an example:

```gdscript
@tool
extends Node
class_name NestedGroups

func _get_property_list() -> Array:
	return [
		{
			"name": "hello_son/hi_dad",
			"type": TYPE_NIL,
			"usage": PROPERTY_USAGE_GROUP,
		},
		{
			"name": "hello_son/uh...",
			"type": TYPE_STRING,
			"hint": PROPERTY_HINT_MULTILINE_TEXT,
		}
	]

func _get(property: StringName) -> Variant:
	if property == "hello_son/uh...":
		return "Dad, you already said hello."
	return null
```

![Troubled family conversation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/583e650c-4ed9-4991-98b2-71a36d5123ac)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Here's the [MRP file](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19717777/propertygroupnestingbug.zip). It contains just a single scene with a node in it that uses the code I posted earlier.

summary: SubViewports scale incorrectly if their size is not divisible by window scale.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4, 4.5-dev @ a210fe6d, #104602 applied on top of a210fe6d

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) UHD Graphics (CML GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10510U CPU @ 1.80GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

SubViewports scale incorrectly if their size is not divisible by the SubViewportContainer's stretch shrink or the window scale.

Because SubViewportContainers base their resizing on the integer valued size property of SubViewports (and  `size_2d_override` is not even available as a `Vector2` outside the class currently), the issue from #102656 is reproducible by placing a low resolution `SubViewport` inside a full screen `SubViewportContainer`.

As far as I understand, this is a very common use case for 2D pixelart games.

Related to #104601, but isn't directly fixed by #104602.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa1388f1-a4cd-4299-b668-26973dee7b05

### Steps to reproduce

- Put a `SubViewport` inside a `SubViewportContainer`
- Set the anchor mode of the `SubViewportContainer` to full rect, so it scales with the window size
- Turn on `Stretch` in the `SubViewportContainer`
- Set either the `SubViewportContainer`'s `Stretch Shrink` property, or the global window scale to larger than 1
- Resize the game window, watch the content in the `SubViewport` jiggle.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[subviewport-sizing-MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19712674/subviewport-sizing-MRP.zip)

### Issue description

I've been setting XROrigin3D & XRCamera3D's in projects I want develop for both XR and desktop, in this case, a 3rd person shooter. In 4.4, the camera is offset from the player as expected, but when the project is run in 4.4.1, the XRCamera3D's local position is snapped back to (0, 0, 0). Disabling Physics Interpolation prevents this.

I should say this is a pretty weird thing to do, and offsetting the XR camera only and not the XR origin was a mistake that came from me never testing the project again in XR once I switched from 1st to 3rd person, but this admittedly cursed setup didn't break in 4.4 and earlier.

Likely caused by: #103233 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add an XROrigin3D and an XRCamera3D child.
2. Move the XRCamera3D in the scene.
3. Enable Physics interpolation.

description.text:

Here's a [tiny_project](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19705122/tiny_project.zip) that demonstrates the issue and the fix when working: To test, run the "Play Custom Scene" tool menu item.

summary: Background becomes semi-transparent after Alt+Tabbing in Fullscreen with Per-Pixel Transparency enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3.stable
4.4 stable
4.4.1 stable


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

I’m not entirely sure if this is a bug or expected behavior, so please let me know if I’ve misunderstood something.

With **Per-Pixel Transparency** enabled in the Project Settings, I’m creating a game that allows switching between normal fullscreen mode and desktop overlay mode. The functionality works as expected in most cases. However, when the game is in fullscreen mode and I Alt+Tab while keeping the game window visible, the sky becomes semi-transparent. It appears as though the blend mode is set to "Add" or a similar effect, making the sky look incorrect.
This only happens in Forward+, if I change to Compatibility renderer, it works as expected.
With V-Sync disabled, the background becomes semi-transparent after Alt+Tabbing in fullscreen mode. 
With V-Sync enabled, the background stays opaque in fullscreen after Alt+Tabbing, but in desktop mode, it fails to become transparent as expected. (Already mentioned here https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/65487 )


Here’s the weird part: when I record with Streamlabs OBS, the sky looks totally fine, all opaque like it should be. But on my screen after Alt+Tabbing, it’s see-through, so something’s off with the display.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9432cfa5-5b3d-407c-9169-81a74937657a)
I get the same results if I change from Sky to Clear Color, Custom color in the World Environment. But not in canvas mode.
Also tried D3D12 but no luck.

I’ve read other issues related to semi-transparent windows and Per-Pixel Transparency but couldn’t find any mentioning alt+tabbing. Apologies if this is a duplicate, but I thought it's worth reporting just in case.

Heres the code but probably better to download the MRP project and test for yourself.
```gdscript

func _ready():
	set_fullscreen_mode()

func _input(event):
	if event.is_action_pressed("debug"): #Keybind is T
		if current_state == GameState.FULLSCREEN:
			set_desktop_mode()
		else:
			set_fullscreen_mode()

func set_fullscreen_mode():
	get_window().transparent = false
	DisplayServer.window_set_mode(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN) #Have tried different full screen modes but no luck.
	#DisplayServer.window_set_mode(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN) #Sky becomes transparent right away without alt tabbing.
	current_state = GameState.FULLSCREEN

func set_desktop_mode():
	get_window().transparent = true
	DisplayServer.window_set_mode(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED)
	current_state = GameState.DESKTOP

```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project.
2. Enable Per-Pixel Transparency
3. Disable V-Sync (Transparency wont work if V-Sync is enabled)
4. Set up a basic scene with default camera and World Environment.
5. Enable fullscreen
6. Run project and alt tab to different window.
7. Notice the game in the background now has a semi transparent background.





### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Debug keybind to switch screen mode is "T"
[transparent_MRP_441.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19702272/transparent_MRP_441.zip)

summary: Add enable checkboxes to editor sections

description.text: Supersedes #81630 Implements: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/4173 Creates support for checkable property groups through the `PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE` property hint that is given to the `enabled` boolean. This property hint modifies the (sub)group to display a checkbox on its right. When the checkbox is clicked, the linked property is enabled and the group is unfolded. This PR also hides the corresponding "enable" property when it is inside a checkable group, saving vertical screen space. This PR also makes the change that when a property group does not have any children, then it is unable to be unfolded and does not draw the unfolding arrow. This can be seen in the `Rotation Smoothing` property group in the example images. | Before (4.4, `Limit` didn't have an enable option yet) | After | | ------------- | ------------- | | | | As opposed to #81630, this PR chooses to have the checkboxes align to the right. This change makes the position of the text consistent, as before the text would jump to the right when the group was checkable.

summary: Godot hangs when there is a change to USB devices

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Originally happened in released game running Godot 4.3.* (Nomad Idle)
- Verified behaviour locally using Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Windows 11 Pro - Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.mono.official.49a5bc7b6 - Vulkan 1.3.302 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: AMD - AMD Radeon RX 9070

### Issue description

Whenever there is an alteration of USB devices on the host system it causes Godot to hang and in some instances be unrecoverable and need manually closing. I have been unable to get a crash report as it just locks up and refuses to close and the logs show nothing, happy to get more information if someone can provide instructions on how to get info when its hung as editor is also unresponsive during this time.

In my specific scenario I am using a USB switcher to switch a wired USB Keyboard/Mouse between two host PCs, switching away from the PC running Godot is not an issue, but when I switch the USB devices back to the pc running Godot the player hangs and is often unrecoverable, I have had someone separately reproduce the hanging by plugging in a USB gamepad while a Godot game (Nomad Idle) is running, they were unable to get an unrecoverable hang, but they reported it hangs for a few seconds whenever they plug in their USB device.

There have been related issues over the years but they seem to have been marked as fixed:
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/36266
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/17453

It is anecdotal but it seems more likely to enter an unrecoverable hang when its under more load, so I am unsure if there is some internal race condition.

### Steps to reproduce

- Load Godot Editor (ideally with something moving in scene to visually see it hang)
- Run the scene
- Plug in a USB device (for me a keyboard/mouse, for others a gamepad)
- The scene in the player hangs for a second or two

If you are unable to get it to be unrecoverable try to put the scene under load and try to plug the USB device in.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

A fresh Godot project should show same issue

summary: Godot 4.4.stable NoiseTexture2D cannot return an image with get_image() on ready

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4 stable, 4.2.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.2.1.stable/Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10.0.26100 - GLES3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 Threads)

### Issue description

`NoiseTexture2D` doesn't return an image object from `get_image()` during `_ready()` the first time the Node is created. If you `await texture.changed` before the first call, `get_image()` will return an Image properly. However, this workaround isn't ideal, since if the Node is created again beyond this first time, the `changed` signal won't be called again due to the `NoiseTexture2D` being instantiated already.

Code Example:
```gdscript
# Called when the node enters the scene tree for the first time.
func _ready():
	print(texture.get_image())
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/520378a1-2693-4947-9695-d35f8f675403)

This seems to be the same issue as #60333, just with a different Texture2D type.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a TextureRect
2. Assign a NoiseTexture2D to the texture
3. Add the line `print(texture.get_image())` to the _ready function
4. Run the project
5. Output will show `<Object#null>`
6. Add `await texture.changed` above the print
7. Output will now show the `Image`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[NoiseTexture2D MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19697660/NoiseTexture2D.MRP.zip)

summary: "Visible Collision Shapes" debug option has an important performance regression since Godot 4.4

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in 4.4.1.stable. There is a clear performance regression (and almost surely the same for 4.4.stable)

It was working fine in 4.3.stable.

### System information

Windows 10 pro 22H2 - Godot 4.4.1.stable - Forward+ - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT

### Issue description

My blocky/voxel based game can render tens of thousands of collision shapes when enabling "Visible Collision Shapes".

As soon as I tried to upgrade from 4.3 to 4.4 I noticed that the performance with "Visible Collision Shapes" (essential at least in my case to debug stuff) was running the game at very low speeds, so much that I couldn't test my game properly anymore and I rolled back to 4.3 and I stayed there for a while since I was convinced that the problem was that the new debug fill takes a toll in performance and it wasn't straight forward to disable debug_fill for all my collision shapes (it is enabled by default). I opened a proposal for ignoring the debug_fill property globally for those that have games in which it causes trouble https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11913

Now, with 4.4.1 I decided to give it a try again. I made sure that all the collisionshape3D nodes have no debug_fill enabled and I tested. It wasn't as bad as before (with the new debug_fill enabled) but I still noticed about half of the FPS when compared with 4.3. I also checked that when "Visible Collision Shapes" is not enabled then the performance is actually a bit higher in 4.4.1 so the issue is definitely with rendering the "Visible Collision Shapes" when testing.

I provide a very simple MRP that makes the issue obvious I think. In my computer, in equal conditions, with the 15000 collisions rendered by the script, there is an almost exact halving of the performance in Godot 4.4.1. 15000 collisions might sound like a crazy number but I actually use it (and need it unless I find a better way) in the game that I am developing and Godot moves it with no issues at 400+ FPS (when "Visible Collision Shapes" is off) so this is a real scenario, at least for me.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fcf1bbb9-ba19-4ae2-8635-60eff1452146)

### Steps to reproduce

Download both these files and unzip them:
collisions performance MRP-4_3.zip
collisions performance MRP-4_4_1.zip

1. Open collisions performance MRP-4_3 project with the Godot 4.3 binary. **Enable Debug->Visible Collision Shapes**
2. Open collisions performance MRP-4_4_1 project with the Godot 4.4.1 binary. **Enable Debug->Visible Collision** Shapes (and also ideally disable "Embed Game on Next Play" from the "Game" tab, that's just so it is easier to do a side by side comparision.
3. Now with both Godot projects open run each of them and place each window side by side so you have the exact same view, resolution, etc. 
4. Make sure not to have any of the 2 windows active (click any other window of your computer) because at least Windows will prioritize more the active window and maybe give more GPU / CPU resources to it.

The script already takes care of disabling "debug fill" property for all the collision shapes in the 4.4.1 project so the conditions are the same as in the 4.3 project.
There are basic camera controls in the MRP but by default the mouse is not captured so the camera is in a fix position so it is easy to compare both projects.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[collisions performance MRP-4_4_1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19688865/collisions.performance.MRP-4_4_1.zip)
[collisions performance MRP-4_3.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19688864/collisions.performance.MRP-4_3.zip)

summary: Storing a PackedScene in a variable loads all of its textures into VRAM

description.text:
### Tested versions


Reproducible in 4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 (nvidia; 565.77) - AMD Ryzen 5 2600 Six-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

When storing a PackedScene in a variable, all the textures of that scene are loaded into video memory. I don't think this is a desirable behaviour, you can end up with a lot of VRAM consumption without knowing why. When I store a reference to a scene, I don't expect anything from that scene to be loaded into memory until I instantiate it.

In my game I have a scene associated with each type of thing, and all of the scenes' textures are loaded into memory when the game starts. This is obviously not ideal. I will have to find a way to circumvent this by not storing PackedScene references directly as variables, which is quite annoying.

This issue could actually be extended to any reference to a texture. Following my case, I have hundreds of resources representing types of things, each one with multiple icons assigned. Even though these icons are not being rendered anywhere, they're still loaded into video memory from the moment I load the resources that reference them. So I either remove all the texture variables and instead store an indirect reference to them (such as a path) or have them loaded in memory at all times.

Anyway, I think the most pressing issue is with scenes, since they are more prone to contain many/big textures. Imagine having a level that stores the next level as a PackedScene variable. You'd have all the next level's textures loaded into VRAM when playing the first, that is ridiculous.

### Steps to reproduce

- Put a PackedScene variable in a script.
- Assign it a scene that uses some texture.
- Load a scene that uses the first script.
- See in the Video RAM debugger that the texture from the reference scene is loaded, even though the scene hasn't been instantiated.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)
 
Just press play and open the VRAM usage in the debugging panel, you'll see that `icon.svg` is loaded in memory when the scene that uses it isn't instantiated.

[PackedScene texture VRAM test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19682538/PackedScene.texture.VRAM.test.zip)

summary: `Input.warp_mouse()` does not work on xwayland

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.stable, 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.dev2. It probably happens on all other versions too.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 24 Mar 2025 06:33:36 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

`Input.warp_mouse()` does not work on xwayland. Here's an issue in the SDL tracker that talks about this problem and a possible workaround https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/issues/9539 and the commit that implements it https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/commit/863d4a0f2ad22ff1460285e3084ff225e44eb49c

On Wayland the problem is already known and noted in this comment https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/c374ce211c00279ac4a69aaaa12b5ff12d7cf6da/platform/linuxbsd/wayland/display_server_wayland.cpp#L435-L440

I tested the workaround listed in the SDL issue using GDScript and it seems to work (although the mouse visibility flickers sometimes) The mouse jumping sometimes is already reported in this issue https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/80008.

### Steps to reproduce

I've attached a MRP with the following code:

```gdscript
extends Node


@onready var label: Label = $Label


var warp_hack = false


func _ready() -> void:
	label.text = "Warp hack: %s" % warp_hack


func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed(&"ui_accept"):
		warp_hack = not warp_hack
		label.text = "Warp hack: %s" % warp_hack

	var vector = Input.get_vector(&"ui_left", &"ui_right", &"ui_up", &"ui_down")

	if not vector.is_equal_approx(Vector2.ZERO):
		var pos = get_window().get_mouse_position() + vector * 500 * delta

		if warp_hack:
			Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_HIDDEN

		Input.warp_mouse(pos)

		if warp_hack:
			Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE

```

You can toggle the workaround by pressing `enter`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[xwayland-warp-mouse_2025-04-10_07-10-56.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19678287/xwayland-warp-mouse_2025-04-10_07-10-56.zip)

summary: Mouse Recursive Behavior does not ignore mouse and related issues

description.text:
### Tested versions Godot v4.5.dev (af2c71397) master ### System information Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads) ### Issue description - See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97495 #### Does not ignore mouse in most cases `get_mouse_filter()` was just replaced with `get_mouse_filter_with_recursive()`, but there are many places in Viewport that access `control->data.mouse_filter` which has not been replaced. This leads to places where disabling the mouse filter (effectively forcing mouse filter ignore) does not work. - All Mouse events are still received, both motion and button. - `NOTIFICATION_MOUSE_ENTER/EXIT_SELF` notifications are still received. - Tooltips still appear. - Drag and Drop still works. - Controls set to Stop still block focus getting to parents. - Cursor shape is still changed. - Moving mouse to a non-overlapping child does not send the mouse exit notification. #### Other issues Getting mouse filter with recursive when it is removed from the tree uses the value from its parent when it was last cached, but it should treat inherit as enabled in that case. `mouse_recursive_behavior` can be set to 3, which is outside the valid enum range. The tab container's `tab_focus_mode` is just a proxy for the internal tab bar's focus mode, so it shouldn't use the 'with recursive' version. `Control::grab_focus` does not check the recursive behavior, so Controls can still grab focus when they shouldn't be able to. #### Confusing name and documentation The name is somewhat confusing. Enabling and disabling "Mouse recursive behavior" implies that the recursion is being enabled and disabled, but it is actually enabling and disabling the mouse for itself and children. The description is unclear about it affecting the current Control. The description for `mouse_recursive_behavior` only mentions children, but the enum description says it does affect itself. The enum being shared makes the description harder to understand with terms like "associated behavior", and there is no need to share the enum. Ideally it would affect the default `get_mouse_filter` so that it is opt-out, but that would cause issues when trying to use the property. I don't think anything can be done about this besides encouraging using the `get_mouse_filter_with_recursive` function instead of the property. The function name `get_mouse_filter_with_recursive` sounds odd since `recursive` is an adjective so it shouldn't be used like that if possible. These all apply to the focus mode too.
To fix these, I think it should be renamed to `mouse_behavior_recursive`, `get_mouse_filter_with_override` and `focus_behavior_recursive`, `get_focus_with_override`. The behavior would stay the same but it should be more clear. This way it is the mouse behavior and focus behavior that is being enabled/disabled, and it acts as an override when getting the mouse filter and focus mode. Though it is not perfect, so I'm open to better suggestions for the name. Since this was added recently, changing this would only break compat with 4.5.dev versions. I will open a PR for this. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Add a Control and a child Control. 2. Set the parent `mouse_recursive_behavior` to Disabled. 3. Add script to the child to print on mouse events in `_gui_input`. 4. Set child's tooltip, cursor, etc. 5. The child should not receive any mouse events, show tooltip, change cursor, etc but it does. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mouse_recursive_mode_test_2025-04-07.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19639726/mouse_recursive_mode_test_2025-04-07.zip)

### Issue description

Accessing the `owner` property from a script attached to a Node (e.g.: "MovementComponent" node) that is part of the original Scene (e.g.: "Entity" scene) returns the root Node of that SceneInstance (e.g.: "Entity" node), while accessing the `owner` property from a script attached to a Node (e.g.: "InputComponent") that was added to the original Scene (e.g.: "Entity") via the "Editable Children" option returns the root Node of the Scene the SceneInstance is a part of (e.g.: "World").

summary: Moving imported blend file and saved animations causes bugged behavior with underlying animation library

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.stable and 4.4.1.stable (8928f5cde).

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono (8928f5cde) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

I have a .blend file with a model and an animation. I've set up the animation to be saved to a resource file so I can add custom tracks / make changes.

res://Wheel.blend, saving animation to res://RollForward.res
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/88a817d4-f234-4703-8c42-d2aa7cd65190)

This works as expected, and it creates an animation library with my single animation that correctly references the saved animation resource.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ae5214f-3ac8-4e30-a1dc-8f72e44a6465)

After moving the blend and animations resource to a new path, I want to update the animation file path. If I try to do this though, it tells me that it will overwrite the existing resource file: 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/729b6d8e-43a8-4fd3-8ff6-6996d559b5b8)

Even if I hit okay for this and reimport the blend file, the underlying animation library loses the reference to the saved animation file (and also loses all custom tracks I made in Godot directly!) However, the import dialog says that it should be saving to the file: 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab2234b1-6d4f-497e-a7f0-fd4d36af4caa)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72d79b0c-4002-4ebd-b132-5d7150f83aeb)

I would expect that after moving the files and updating the file paths in the import settings, it would use the animation resource file that was referenced from the import settings without overwriting the existing file. However, after moving the reference is broken and there doesn't seem to be a way to fix it aside from saving the animation to a new file and redoing all my in-editor changes.

Currently to work around this I've had to move away from using the .blend files directly and individually manage the animations / animation libraries.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a blend file with a model and animation and save it into the Godot folder.
2. From the advanced import settings for the blend file, set the animation save path to save the animation to a specific path.
3. Reimport the blend file.
4. Move the blend file and saved animation resource into a subfolder.
5. Go into the advanced import settings for the blend file again and update the animation Save to File path to the moved animation resource.
6. Reimport the blend file.  

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

To reproduce with this example:
1. Move Wheel.blend and RollForward.res files into a new folder.
2. Go to the import dialog for Wheel.blend, update the path for the RollForward animation to the new animation resource path.
3. Reimport Wheel.blend.

summary: Use a dialog when there are too many Resource Picker popup menu items

description.text: - *Production edit: closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/43, closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3430, closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3333* This PR adds an editor setting to set a threshold for the number of items that should appear in the popup menu for the Resource Picker. If the items exceed this value, it opens them as a searchable dialog instead: ## Vids and Pics https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ca7c1dab-6264-4d53-aa32-98ee27416cfa > [!NOTE] > For demonstration purposes the threshold is set to `10` in this video, but by default is `30`. So normally the dialog wouldn't trigger for `Mesh`. Afaik only a `Resource` `@export` would exceed `30`. ## Details * The threshold option can be set to `0` to always open the dialog, or `-1` to disable the dialog all together. * A popup menu is still used when the arrow button is clicked; I think it would be jarring if the button with a down-arrow didn't create a popup menu underneath it. So basically the dialog only appears when creating a new resource and clicking ``. * The dialog is implemented by scanning the contents of the provided `PopupMenu` and converting its entries into list items. While this may seem a little hacky, I thought it would be a good way to let this behavior become reusable in the future for any other dynamically sized `PopupMenu`s. It also means the behavior for generating the entries in the first place doesn't need to be duplicated. ## To Be Discussed * Maybe the popup menu should also contain a button to open as a searchable dialog regardless of the size? * The `ItemList` widget used in the dialog doesn't have any obvious "separator" equivalent to replicate the separators from the `PopupMenu`. If we want to keep the separators, I could make a specialized `ItemList`-lookalike Control similar to how the `Quick Open` does it and add them that way, but I wanted to get feedback first before I put that much work into it. Thanks for reading!

summary: Embedded `PopupMenu` with `visible` set to `true` in the inspector can't `grab_focus()`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable and 4.5.dev2

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 24 Mar 2025 06:33:36 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

For some reason, when an embedded `PopupMenu` starts visible the flag [`Windows.FLAG_NO_FOCUS`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_window.html#class-window-constant-flag-no-focus) is set to `true` so it won't grab the focus. The items won't highlight when the mouse is over them and won't react to mouse clicks.

The only place where I can see this happening is here https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/96579d139951262f7c766fa0ea88583c85aca674/scene/gui/popup_menu.cpp#L3026 which make me think that `is_embedded()` returns `false` when initializing the scene and the flag ends being set to `true`.

If the `PopupMenu` starts hidden and you try to `show()` or set `visible` to `true` in `_ready()` the `PopupMenu` won't be shown. If you wait a frame with `await get_tree().process_frame` the `PopupMenu` will show and works fine.

If the `PopupMenu` starts visible and you call `show()` or set `visible` to `true` then the `PopupMenu` will work without needing to wait a frame. You can also set [`Window.unfocusable`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_window.html#class-window-property-unfocusable) to `false` and the `PopupMenu` will work fine (this property just changes the `FLAG_NO_FOCUS` flag).

### Steps to reproduce

I've attached a MRP that connects the signals `Window.mouse_entered`,  `Window.focus_entered`, and `PopupMenu.id_pressed`

It also has the following commented out:

```gdscript
	# If the PopupMenu is already visible in the editor then this will make it work
	#$PopupMenu.visible = true
	# If the PopupMenu is hidden in the editor the popup won't show unless yo do:
	#await get_tree().process_frame
	#$PopupMenu.visible = true
```

If you make the popup visible in the editor and run the project you will notice that:

- `mouse_entered` is fired
- `focus_entered` or `id_pressed` won't be fired. The items won't be highlighted and won't react to clicks.

If you then un-comment the first part you will notice that everything works fine.

Then, you can hide the popup in the editor and run the project again and you'll notice that:

- `focus_entered` is fired
- The popup won't be visible.

If you then, comment the first part and un-comment the second you'll notice that everything works fine again.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Newly created scripts being misclassified as built-in

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1 and master [4248411baf]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (4248411ba) - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 27 Feb 2025 18:09:27 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (RADV NAVI22) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When attaching a new script to a node (e.g. named World) the script is correctly written to disk with the path`res://world.gd`. However, the attached script is misclassified as a **built-in script**. In the script editor it shown up as `world.tscn::GDScript_3wlsy`. Here's what it looks like in the tscn file:
```
[sub_resource type="GDScript" id="GDScript_3wlsy"]
script/source = "extends Node2D
```
**Note:** Modifying or saving this script does not update the file on disk, instead, changes are only applied to the .tscn file.

**Expected Behavior:**

The newly created script should be recognized as an external script with the correct file path, not as a built-in script.

**Cause:**

It seems like the issue is triggered by a call to `EditorFileSystem::update_files(...)` right after the script is saved.

From what I can tell, `update_files(...)` re-scans the file system and reloads the script from disk using `ResourceLoader`. During that process:

- A new instance of the script is loaded from disk.
- That instance gets registered in the `ResourceCache` under the saved path (like `res://world.gd`).
- Then, to avoid path duplication, the original in-memory script (the one that was just saved) has its path cleared.

So now, the script object that gets passed around (for example, to `script_created` signal listeners like `SceneTreeDock::_script_created()`) no longer has a path — and since `get_path()` returns an empty string, it's treated as a **built-in** script.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project
2. Create a node
3. Click "Attach a new or existing script to the selected node."
4. Click "Create"

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I'm not sure if there's something with my system that causes this behavior, so I don't think providing a reproducible example would help, it would just be a file with a script called "world.gd" and a built in script on the World node.
